implementation.summary: $28M authorized C78. $4.2M approved for disbursement C81. $0 delivered as of C84. 295 applications pending.
pendingItems: Vega committee review — agenda item 4 (cycle 89, September 8)
implementation.milestoneNotes: $28M authorized C78; $4.2M approved C81; 0 disbursed C84; 295 applications waiting
implementation.newsroomNote: Mara audit corrected: $4.2M IS approved for disbursement, not zero. But zero has been delivered. The gap between approved and delivered is the story. September 8th is the deadline.

implementation.summary: 45-day implementation window active. 3 pilot districts named: D1, D3, D5. No hiring criteria released.
implementation.milestoneNotes: Passed 5-4 C82; 3 pilot districts named (D1, D3, D5); 45-day clock running
implementation.newsroomNote: Mara audit corrected: 3 pilot districts ARE named (D1, D3, D5), not none. Every civic piece touching OARI must carry countdown: Day X of 45.

implementation.summary: Scope released C83. Developer RFP and selection underway. Council vote (simple majority 5-4) targeted C86. $22M Housing Trust Fund commitment requires council appropriation.
implementation.milestoneNotes: Visioning complete; conceptual plan approved; BART MOU negotiated; scope released C83; developer RFP active
implementation.nextScheduledAction: Council vote — $22M Housing Trust Fund appropriation
keyContacts: Shawn Nguyen (43, construction laborer, local hire)
implementation.newsroomNote: $230M transit-oriented development at Fruitvale BART. 420 units, 67% affordable. 50% retail reserved for Fruitvale-based or Latino-owned businesses. Feeds Oakland Youth Apprenticeship Pipeline. Infrastructure sequencing with Baylight flagged by planning office.

keyContacts: Patricia Nolan (home health aide, waiting on services)
Andre Lee (55, Temescal, active — health fair attendee)
implementation.newsroomNote: Community health access is the human angle. Track wait times and service availability. Mara corrected Andre Lee: 55/Temescal/active, not 65/Adams Point/retired.

implementation.summary: Bond passed 6-3 C83. TIF Ordinance executed C84. Remediation Bond executed C84. Construction mobilization request filed C85. Both legal instruments cleared — project advancing to site prep.
implementation.milestoneNotes: Bond passed 6-3 C83; TIF executed C84; Remediation Bond executed C84; mobilization request filed C85
implementation.nextScheduledAction: Baylight anchor tenant disclosure + fiscal close end Q3
pendingItems: Anchor tenant disclosure and fiscal close (end Q3)
First contract award — investigative angle: who gets it?
keyContacts: Dr. Simone Ellis (City Legal Counsel — TIF language)
Keisha Ramos (Director, Baylight Redevelopment Authority — mobilization lead)
implementation.newsroomNote: Both legal instruments cleared C84 — project is no longer blocked. Mobilization request filed C85. The story shifts from 'will it happen' to 'who benefits first.' Private sector activity preceded public filings. Major investigative angle: who gets the first contract?
